key_insight: A zero-setup segmentation pipeline can turn reconstructed X-ray tomography volumes into interpretable multi-phase masks without user prompting or dataset-specific retraining.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-real-time feedback at beamlines, helping researchers quickly assess scan quality, morphology, porosity, and attenuation before moving on to the next experiment.
method: Material-agnostic mask preparation creates standard regions: background, sample, bright, dark-gray, light-gray, and porosity.
A pretrained semantic segmentation network predicts phase masks directly on new scans.
Outputs can be used immediately or later refined for application-specific model tuning.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent, physically meaningful segmentations on held-out synchrotron micro-CT images and qualitative testing on additional datasets, with better performance than intensity-based thresholding.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on reconstruction quality and imaging conditions, so unusual materials or severe artifacts may require manual refinement.
paper_title: From Reconstruction to Interpretation: Zero-Setup Multi-Phase Segmentation of X-ray Tomography Data
